lines: Chatter, Conversation And Commentary
It's the third year of the Dick Vermeil era.  The Chiefs' offense,
the best in the NFL last year, is in tact.  Their defense has been beefed up,

blocks.text:
led by Shawn Barber, Ryan Sims, and the returning Jerome Woods.  And
    except for cantankerous Ike Gardner, we all believe the Chiefs will win the
    season opener against San Diego, Sunday out at Arrowheaaaaaad.
    But,  Ike is Ike.  Even his closest friends don't understand why he has
    taken such an opposing view, when it comes to the Chiefs.  Understandably,
    he is a frustrated, loyal Chiefs' fan. In fact, his season ticket seats are on the
    50-yard-line, directly behind the Chiefs' bench.  That way, he can harass, meddle
    with, and help coach the Chiefs. Sincerely though, Ike just wants the Chiefs to
    return to the old glory days.  And, it has been 33 years, since the Chiefs have
    been  theto the Super Bowl.  Thus, Ike represents a group of old-timers, who just
    want the Chiefs to compete well and be respected, again.

blocks.text:
So, next week, Monday Niters will all look into the future, and
    predict just  how many games the Chiefs will win in 2003.  That discussion will
    take place this Monday night, just as it has for the last 34 years.  Ten friends,
    sitting down together, eating a kingly-prepared meal, taking a quick nap, and just
    talking endlessly about their favorite pastime, football.  Even, Ike joins in,
    occasionally.
    Editor's Note: Of course, Ike continues toes to give up on our beloved
   Chiefs even before they take their first series in the new season. Well, ol' Ike,
    someday you'll learn. Of courssssssssssshful will show you just how good this 2003 bunch will be. Come on Ike, give us some love here.
e the real "Chiefs" faithful will show you old-timer

full_prose_text:
π  Kansas City Chiefs vs. San Diego Chargers
   12 p.m. KCTV 5
            Game Prediction (Wins In Parenthesis)

Drew Brees

Chatter, Conversation And Commentary
    It's the third year of the Dick Vermeil era.  The Chiefs' offense,
    the best in the NFL last year, is in tact.  Their defense has been beefed up,

led by Shawn Barber, Ryan Sims, and the returning Jerome Woods.  And
    except for cantankerous Ike Gardner, we all believe the Chiefs will win the
    season opener against San Diego, Sunday out at Arrowheaaaaaad.
    But,  Ike is Ike.  Even his closest friends don't understand why he has
    taken such an opposing view, when it comes to the Chiefs.  Understandably,
    he is a frustrated, loyal Chiefs' fan. In fact, his season ticket seats are on the
    50-yard-line, directly behind the Chiefs' bench.  That way, he can harass, meddle
    with, and help coach the Chiefs. Sincerely though, Ike just wants the Chiefs to
    return to the old glory days.  And, it has been 33 years, since the Chiefs have
    been  theto the Super Bowl.  Thus, Ike represents a group of old-timers, who just
    want the Chiefs to compete well and be respected, again.
    	Because he has been disappointed so many times, he has lost faith and

So, next week, Monday Niters will all look into the future, and
    predict just  how many games the Chiefs will win in 2003.  That discussion will
    take place this Monday night, just as it has for the last 34 years.  Ten friends,
    sitting down together, eating a kingly-prepared meal, taking a quick nap, and just
    talking endlessly about their favorite pastime, football.  Even, Ike joins in,
    occasionally.
    Editor's Note: Of course, Ike continues toes to give up on our beloved
   Chiefs even before they take their first series in the new season. Well, ol' Ike,
    someday you'll learn. Of courssssssssssshful will show you just how good this 2003 bunch will be. Come on Ike, give us some love here.
e the real "Chiefs" faithful will show you old-timer
    just  how good this 2003  bunch will be. Come on Ike, give us some love.

By Jim "Grand Dad" Nunnelly
